2603.26738.title: SleepVLM: Explainable and Rule-Grounded Sleep Staging via a Vision-Language Model
2603.26738.paper_summary: While automated sleep staging has achieved expert-level accuracy, its clinical adoption is hindered by a lack of auditable reasoning. We introduce SleepVLM, a rule-grounded vision-language model (VLM) designed to stage sleep from multi-channel polysomnography (PSG) waveform images while generating clinician-readable rationales based on American Academy of Sleep Medicine (AASM) scoring criteria. Utilizing waveform-perceptual pre-training and rule-grounded supervised fine-tuning, SleepVLM achieved Cohen's kappa scores of 0.767 on an held out test set (MASS-SS1) and 0.743 on an external cohort (ZUAMHCS), matching state-of-the-art performance. Expert evaluations further validated the quality of the model's reasoning, with mean scores exceeding 4.0/5.0 for factual accuracy, evidence comprehensiveness, and logical coherence. By coupling competitive performance with transparent, rule-based explanations, SleepVLM may improve the trustworthiness and auditability of automated sleep staging in clinical workflows. To facilitate further research in interpretable sleep medicine, we release MASS-EX, a novel expert-annotated dataset.
2603.26738.paper_summary_zh: 雖然自動化睡眠分期已達到專家級的準確性，但其臨床應用受到缺乏可審計推理的阻礙。我們介紹了SleepVLM，一種基於規則的視覺-語言模型（VLM），旨在從多通道多導睡眠圖（PSG）波形圖像中進行睡眠分期，同時根據美國睡眠醫學學會（AASM）計分標準生成臨床可讀的推理。利用波形感知預訓練和基於規則的監督微調，SleepVLM在保留的測試集（MASS-SS1）上達到了0.767的Cohen's kappa分數，在外部隊列（ZUAMHCS）上達到了0.743，匹配了最先進的性能。專家評估進一步驗證了模型推理的質量，在事實準確性、證據全面性和邏輯一致性方面的平均分數超過4.0/5.0。通過將競爭性能與透明的基於規則的解釋相結合，SleepVLM可能提高自動化睡眠分期在臨床工作流程中的可信度和可審計性。為了促進可解釋睡眠醫學的進一步研究，我們釋出了MASS-EX，一個新穎的專家標註數據集。
2603.26738.author: Guifeng Deng et.al.
2603.26738.authors: Guifeng Deng, Pan Wang, Jiquan Wang, Shuying Rao, Junyi Xie, Wanjun Guo, Tao Li, Haiteng Jiang
